response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack and web-systems engineer, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is minimal—roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project—far short of the mandated 5–10 years. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. There is no evidence of Windows platform development, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no exposure to fixed-income, capital markets, or investment-banking systems. The brief stint at RIDE Capital involved TypeScript/GraphQL work on investment tooling, which does not constitute capital-markets domain depth. Optional desirable skills such as Summit, Kafka, Python, and Java (beyond minimal mentions) are also absent.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) demonstrating adaptability across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains
Strong CI/CD and DevOps awareness—GitLab, Docker, trunk-based development, and CI pipelines are recurring themes
Demonstrated communication skills through conference talks, workshops, and open-source community engagement
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months, 2014–2015) versus the 5–10 year mandatory requirement
No capital markets, fixed income, or investment-banking domain knowledge; RIDE Capital work was TypeScript-based product engineering
No evidence of SQL Server, Oracle, or Windows platform development—the primary operational environment for this role

key_factors.factor: Capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
